CHS Alumni Weekly Newsletter

Season 2025-26 | Week 20 (Mar 9 to Mar 15)
Matchup Summaries Report Cards Betting Lines Player of the Week Fun Facts What If? Power Rankings Stats Corner Around the NBA Rumor Mill Record Book

Matchup Summaries

Luka my Balls 1910.20 def. Saboner 1778.90

Luka my Balls (16-4): 50 games, 38.20 fppg (G: 41.30 | F: 36.80 | C: 35.00)

Saboner (9-11): 46 games, 38.67 fppg (G: 37.80 | F: 41.20 | C: 38.40)

Season series: Nick 6, Garrett 1 | All-time: Nick leads 46-20

Last week's Betting Lines billed this as a pick'em -- Nick a -105 moneyline favorite with just a 9.5-point spread -- and for most of the week, it lived up to the billing. Garrett actually posted the higher team FPPG (38.67 to 38.20), squeezing more per-game production out of his roster than the six-time champion. But fantasy is a volume game, and Nick's 4-game schedule advantage (50 games played to Garrett's 46) made the difference in a 131.30-point victory. It helped that Luka Doncic (234.55 FP over 3 games) decided to remind everyone why his team bears his name, headlined by a 99.8 FP explosion against Chicago on March 12 -- the highest single-game score of the week across all four teams.

Jalen Johnson (161.90 FP over 3 games) and Ryan Rollins (159.00 FP over 4 games) provided steady complementary scoring, while Nick's guard corps dominated the positional battle at 867.05 total FP. The forward gap was even more lopsided: Nick's 588.80 FP at the position doubled Garrett's 371.00. Garrett's 8 games lost to injury -- compared to just 3 for Nick -- told the story on the margins. Kawhi Leonard (185.45 FP over 4 games) was Garrett's best player and Jalen Brunson (178.70 FP over 4 games) continued his quietly excellent second half, but the depth simply wasn't there to overcome the schedule disparity.

The result snapped Garrett's 4-game win streak, the longest of his season and a run that had included wins over the top two teams in consecutive weeks. Nick now owns 46 of the 66 all-time meetings between these two -- a staggering 70% win rate that remains the league's most lopsided rivalry. With both teams trending hot entering the week (Nick 9% above his season average, Garrett 11% above his), the matchup delivered on its billing as a high-scoring affair -- 3689.10 combined points -- even if the final margin wasn't as razor-thin as the oddsmakers expected.

Big Nik Energy 1633.55 def. Smaxey 1560.55

Big Nik Energy (5-15): 40 games, 40.84 fppg (G: 41.00 | F: 33.20 | C: 51.20)

Smaxey (10-10): 39 games, 40.01 fppg (G: 45.30 | F: 35.00 | C: 35.90)

Season series: Hayden 3, Benton 4 | All-time: Hayden leads 40-26

If you had Hayden circled as a +975 moneyline underdog to take down the -975 favorite, congratulations -- you and absolutely nobody else. Benton entered this one as a 371.5-point favorite with a 90.70% win probability, the most prohibitive line of the season, and Hayden tore it to shreds in a 73.00-point upset that extended Benton's losing streak to 3 games. The architect of the destruction? Nikola Jokic, who turned in one of the most dominant weeks of the entire season: 304.35 FP across 4 games for a 76.09 FPPG, punctuated by an 88.6 FP masterclass against San Antonio on March 12. It was the kind of week where even a 5-15 record can't diminish the fact that Hayden's roster, when healthy-ish, has a genuine superstar advantage.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (189.80 FP over 3 games) added a second elite scoring punch, and Scottie Barnes (135.40 FP over 4 games) rounded out a top three that collectively accounted for 629.55 of Hayden's 1633.55 total. The 51.20 center FPPG was eye-popping -- almost entirely Jokic's doing -- and gave Hayden a massive positional edge that Benton's guard advantage (45.30 to 41.00 FPPG) couldn't overcome. Both teams were ravaged by injuries: Hayden lost 11 games to injury and Benton lost 13, leaving them at 78.4% and 75.0% utilization rates respectively. Benton got strong production from Cade Cunningham (204.05 FP over 4 games) and a resurgent Josh Giddey (197.65 FP over 3 games), but with Tyrese Maxey, Jarrett Allen, and Michael Porter Jr. all sidelined, the supporting cast was stretched too thin.

The loss drops Benton to 10-10 and extends a slide that started in Week 18 -- three straight losses after holding a 10-7 record. For Hayden, it's a rare bright spot in a brutal season, pulling the season series to 3-4 and reinforcing the all-time dominance (40-26) that the standings don't reflect. The upset also has massive draft implications: with all four finishing positions now locked at 100%, Benton's clinching of second place means Hayden will own both the 1st overall pick (his own, via 4th place) and the 2nd overall pick (acquired from Garrett in the Week 14 Curry/Edgecombe trade). It's the kind of draft capital haul that could reshape the league for years.

Report Cards

Hayden (Big Nik Energy) A-

Record: 5-15 | Won vs Benton by 73.00 | 1633.55 FP | +9.3% vs projection | Injuries: 11 | Bench: 0 | Blunders: 0 | Waivers: 1 adds (133.25 FP over 3 games, 44.42 fppg, 133.2 FP per add)

The best week of Hayden's season -- and it wasn't particularly close. A 109.3% efficiency rating means Hayden's healthy players didn't just meet expectations, they blew past them, with Jokic's 304.35 FP accounting for nearly a fifth of the team's total output. The Matas Buzelis waiver add contributed 133.25 FP over 3 games at a sizzling 44.42 FPPG, a shrewd pickup that exemplified the kind of roster management Hayden has lacked at other points this season. The 11 games lost to injury remain a persistent problem -- Hayden's 26.9% season injury burden and 178 games lost to injury in starter slots lead the league -- but this week the available players compensated beautifully. Toppling a -975 moneyline favorite is the kind of result that makes you wonder what this season could have looked like with better health. A 78.4% utilization rate (40 of 51 games) is still low, but when you're extracting 40.84 FPPG from the games you do play, the raw totals hold up. The jump from a C last week to an A- reflects a team that maximized everything it had to work with.

Garrett (Saboner) B+

Record: 9-11 | Lost vs Nick by 131.30 | 1778.90 FP | +4.8% vs projection | Injuries: 8 | Bench: 0 | Blunders: 0 | Waivers: 3 adds (324.15 FP over 11 games, 29.47 fppg, 108.1 FP per add)

A strong week on paper, even in a loss. Garrett posted 1778.90 FP with a 104.8% efficiency rating, and his 38.67 team FPPG actually edged out the champion -- he just couldn't overcome a 4-game schedule deficit (46 games played to Nick's 50). The three waiver additions -- Kyle Filipowski (121.90 FP over 3 games), Immanuel Quickley (119.30 FP over 4 games), and Rudy Gobert (82.95 FP over 4 games) -- combined for 324.15 FP across 11 games, a productive haul that kept the roster functional through 8 games lost to injury. Kawhi Leonard (185.45 FP over 4 games) and Jalen Brunson (178.70 FP over 4 games) anchored the top end, while Donovan Clingan (154.80 FP over 3 games) continued his hot streak. The 4-game win streak is over, but Garrett's recent form -- averaging 1697 over his last 3 weeks, 11% above his season average -- suggests the late-season surge is real even if this week came up short. Zero blunders and clean lineup management deserve credit in a week where navigating injuries was the primary challenge.

Nick (Luka my Balls) B+

Record: 16-4 | Won vs Garrett by 131.30 | 1910.20 FP | +4.4% vs projection | Injuries: 3 | Bench: 0 | Blunders: 0 | Waivers: None

Another week, another win for the six-time champion. Nick's 1910.20 FP was the highest total of any team this week, powered by a Luka Doncic performance (234.55 FP over 3 games, 78.18 FPPG) that included the week's top single-game score -- 99.8 FP against Chicago. The 104.4% efficiency rating marks the third consecutive week Nick has exceeded projections, and a 94.3% utilization rate (50 of 53 games) remains comfortably the league's best. Only 3 games lost to injury this week -- a luxury in a league where everyone else is dealing with double-digit injury losses -- kept the machine humming. Nick's 19.7% season injury burden is the league's lowest by a wide margin. No waiver adds were needed, which is the kind of roster stability that comes from having the league's deepest lineup top to bottom. The scoring trend continues to run hot: an average of 1795.5 FP over the last 3 weeks, 9% above the season average of 1644.9. The B+ rather than an A reflects the context -- beating a depleted Garrett team that was favored by just 9.5 points doesn't move the needle the way a statement win would.

Benton (Smaxey) B

Record: 10-10 | Lost vs Hayden by 73.00 | 1560.55 FP | +4.0% vs projection | Injuries: 13 | Bench: 0 | Blunders: 0 | Waivers: None

The B might seem generous for a team that just lost as a -975 moneyline favorite, but the underlying numbers tell a more sympathetic story. Benton's 104.0% efficiency rating means his healthy players performed well -- the issue was that only 39 of 52 scheduled games were played, a 75.0% utilization rate dragged down by 13 games lost to injury. Cade Cunningham (204.05 FP over 4 games) was excellent, and Josh Giddey (197.65 FP over 3 games) delivered the best week of his recent hot streak. But without Tyrese Maxey, Jarrett Allen, and Michael Porter Jr., Benton was essentially bringing a knife to a gunfight at the forward and center positions -- 349.80 FP at forward (35.00 FPPG) and 394.85 FP at center (35.90 FPPG) simply couldn't keep pace with a Jokic-powered opponent. The 3-game losing streak (Weeks 18-20) is Benton's longest of the season, matching his best win streak of 3 (Weeks 3-5) for a symmetry nobody wanted. Zero blunders and clean lineup decisions mean this week was about health, not mismanagement -- a marked improvement from the blunder-heavy weeks that plagued the middle of the season. Benton's 25.7% season injury burden (164 games lost to injury) now trails only Hayden for the league lead.

Betting Lines

Luka my Balls vs Smaxey
Team Spread Total Moneyline Win Prob Avg Score
Luka my Balls +33.0 o3770 +120 45.64% 1871.32
Smaxey -33.0 u3770 -120 54.36% 1901.05

The regular season finale doubles as a playoff preview, with the 1-seed squaring off against the 2-seed in a matchup that's closer than the standings suggest. The headliner is Luka Doncic (55.9 projected FPPG), coming off a 99.8 FP game and riding a three-week heater, against Cade Cunningham (46.8 projected FPPG) and the steady hand of James Harden (41.6 projected FPPG). Benton is a slight -120 favorite, bolstered by a 3-game schedule edge (53 startable games to Nick's 50) and positional advantages at both guard (+10.06 FPPG) and forward (+6.79 FPPG). But Nick holds the center edge (+5.19 FPPG) and, more importantly, the momentum -- he's averaging 1796 over the last 3 weeks, 9% above his season average, while Benton has been stable but unspectacular at 1496. Michael Porter Jr. could return from his ankle injury as early as March 18, which would give Benton a significant mid-week boost. Nick has won 3 straight in this matchup and leads the season series 4-2, adding a psychological edge to what the numbers call a coin flip.

Big Nik Energy vs Saboner
Team Spread Total Moneyline Win Prob Avg Score
Big Nik Energy +112.0 o3570 +185 35.02% 1732.86
Saboner -112.0 u3570 -185 64.98% 1833.47

The last regular season matchup of the year pits the league's luckiest winner against its unluckiest loser, with third place and fourth place already cemented. The marquee battle features Nikola Jokic (59.9 projected FPPG), fresh off his sixth POTW of the season, against Victor Wembanyama (55.8 projected FPPG) and Kawhi Leonard (42.9 projected FPPG) -- two generational big men headlining what should be the week's most entertaining positional clash at center. Garrett is a comfortable -185 favorite with a 112-point spread, backed by a 4-game schedule edge (53 startable games to Hayden's 49) and advantages at both forward (+101.35 FPPG) and center (+33.92 FPPG). Hayden's guard corps is the one area of strength, holding a massive +65.34 FPPG edge, but that alone may not be enough to offset the depth gap. Garrett is still running hot -- 11% above his season average over the last 3 weeks -- while Hayden sits right around his baseline. Pascal Siakam could return from his knee injury as early as March 18, giving Hayden a potential late-week lift, but with Sabonis and Curry still sidelined for Garrett, both teams enter dinged up. Garrett has won 5 of 6 against Hayden this season, though Hayden still leads the all-time series 34-30 -- a historical edge that hasn't translated to this year's matchups.

Player of the Week

Nikola Jokic

304.35 FP over 4 games (76.09 FPPG) | +27.12% vs projection | 18.63% of team output

There's a reason they call it "Big Nik Energy." Jokic claimed his sixth Player of the Week award of the season -- more than any other player in the league -- with a 304.35 FP masterpiece that was both relentless and efficient. The crown jewel was an 88.6 FP eruption against the Spurs on March 12, but what made this week special was the absence of a bad game: Jokic never dipped below his season average in any of his four appearances. At 76.09 FPPG, he exceeded his projected output by more than 27%, and his 18.63% team contribution carried a Hayden squad that was missing key pieces to a stunning upset of the -975 favorite Smaxey. This is Jokic's sixth POTW this season (Weeks 3, 5, 10, 16, 17, and now 20), a frequency that underscores his status as the most dominant fantasy asset in the league. For Hayden, Jokic's brilliance is the difference between a historically unlucky season and a genuinely bad one -- when the big man goes supernova, this team can beat anyone.

Honorable Mentions: Luka Doncic posted 234.55 FP across 3 games (78.18 FPPG) -- actually outpacing Jokic on a per-game basis, but his 3-game week couldn't match Jokic's cumulative total, even as his 99.8 FP game vs Chicago was the single highest-scoring performance of the week. Cade Cunningham delivered 204.05 FP across 4 games (51.01 FPPG) -- a steady four-game week highlighted by a 61.3 FP outing against Memphis that kept Benton competitive despite a depleted supporting cast.

Fun Facts

  • Luka Doncic exploded for 99.8 fantasy points in a single game for Nick -- the week's highest individual performance and just the latest reminder that when Luka goes nuclear, the scoreboard melts.
  • Nikola Jokic erupted for 88.6 fantasy points in a single game for Hayden, marking the second-highest individual game of the week and fueling a POTW performance that powered an enormous upset.
  • Nick's forwards outscored Garrett's by 218 FP this week -- a positional beatdown that helped offset Garrett's advantages at guard and center.
  • Hayden has lost 305 total injury games this season -- 80 more than Nick's league-low 225. The gap between the league's most and least battered rosters continues to widen.
  • Hayden's scoring profile suggests a 9.3-10.7 record, but he sits at 5-15 instead -- a luck index of -4.3, the league's worst this season and the worst single season of Hayden's nine-year career.
  • Nick has now won 46 of 66 all-time meetings against Garrett, a 70% win rate that stands as the most dominant head-to-head record in league history.
  • Hayden has now won 40 of 66 all-time meetings against Benton, a 61% clip that makes it the league's second-most lopsided rivalry -- and one that grew by another chapter with this week's upset.

What If?

No major lineup regrets this week -- in fact, no lineup regrets at all. All four managers posted perfect lineup management: zero bench points, zero blunders, and zero missed opportunities across the board. Nick left nothing on the table. Hayden left nothing on the table. Benton and Garrett? Same story. In a league where blunders have swung matchups and fueled entire newsletter sections this season, Week 20 was a rare week of universal discipline. No swaps would have changed either outcome, and no manager has a single decision to second-guess. Enjoy the clean conscience -- the playoffs start next week.

Power Rankings

Rank Team Record Title Odds Trend
1 Luka my Balls 16-4 100.0% --
2 Big Nik Energy 5-15 0.0% --
3 Saboner 9-11 0.0% --
4 Smaxey 10-10 0.0% --

The standings for the 2025-26 season have officially been locked in place. All four finishing positions are 100% finalized: Nick in first, Benton in second, Garrett in third, and Hayden in fourth. Title odds haven't budged -- Nick holds 100.0% and has since clinching in Week 18, projecting a 16.6-4.4 final record that would cement this as one of the most dominant seasons in league history. His six championships are already more than any other manager, and the 119-75 career record speaks to a dynasty that shows no signs of slowing down. Nick enters the playoffs at 94.0% health with only Anthony Davis still sidelined, and a scoring trend that's been running 9% above his season average over the last three weeks.

Benton clinching second place triggers the offseason's most significant storyline: Hayden now owns both the 1st and 2nd overall picks in the 2026 draft. Hayden's own 4th-place finish delivers the #1 overall selection, and Garrett's 3rd-place finish delivers the #2 overall -- a pick Hayden acquired from Garrett in the Week 14 trade that sent Stephen Curry and mid-round picks for VJ Edgecombe, Garrett's 1st rounder, and Garrett's 5th rounder. Despite a 5-15 season defined by the league's worst luck index (-4.3, the worst of Hayden's nine-year career) and a league-high 26.9% injury burden (305 total injury games, including 178 in starter slots), Hayden is positioned to make a franchise-altering haul this summer. The 76.3 keeper quality score ranks second in the league, with Jokic, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, and a collection of young pieces forming a foundation that two top-of-draft additions could transform overnight.

Garrett finishes third at 9-11, a disappointing result after a 4-game win streak (Weeks 16-19) had briefly reignited hopes of catching Benton. The loss to Nick in Week 20 ended that run, and with Sabonis and Curry still sidelined, the 87.4% current health rate limited what was otherwise a productive week. Still, Garrett's late-season surge -- averaging 1697 over the last 3 weeks, 11% above his season average -- is an encouraging sign heading into the playoffs. His 73.8 keeper quality score, anchored by Wembanyama, Clingan, and Sengun, suggests the center pipeline could be the league's best for years to come. Benton, meanwhile, holds the better record (10-10) but slots in at fourth in the power rankings due to a 3-game losing streak and a 87.1% health rate that has been declining for weeks. The 72.7 keeper quality score trails the field, though the 10.4-10.6 expected record confirms that the .500 finish is right in line with what the numbers expected.

Week 21 is the final regular season week, and the playoff matchups are set: #1 Nick vs #4 Hayden, and #2 Benton vs #3 Garrett. Both matchups are rematches from earlier this season -- Nick has dominated Hayden 6-1 in the season series, while Benton holds a 4-3 edge over Garrett. The regular season finale doubles as a dress rehearsal, with Nick facing Benton and Hayden taking on Garrett in what amounts to a crossover preview of the potential championship week to come.

Top Available Free Agents

Player NBA Team Position Proj FPPG Games This Week Games Next Week
Darius Garland LAC PG 36.10 4 0
Jrue Holiday POR PG,SG 34.47 4 0
Payton Pritchard BOS PG 34.27 4 0
Reed Sheppard HOU PG,SG 32.68 4 0
Zion Williamson NOP SF,PF,C 31.48 4 0

Luka Doncic's 99.8 FP game against Chicago on March 12 was the week's top individual performance and the closest any player has come to a triple-digit fantasy outing this season -- his second honorable mention nod in three weeks after winning POTW outright in Weeks 15 and 18. Jokic appearing twice in the top 5 (88.6 and 81.95) underscores the kind of sustained dominance that has carried Hayden through one of the league's most injury-ravaged campaigns. On the waiver wire, Hayden's Matas Buzelis pickup led all adds at 44.42 FPPG, while Garrett's three-add strategy netted 324.15 FP across 11 games -- a volume play that kept his roster functional through significant injury losses. The free agent pool is thin heading into the final week, with all five top options showing zero games next week.

Around the NBA

  • Luka Drops 51 on the Bulls, Then Hits OT Game-Winner to Stun Nuggets
    Nick's franchise cornerstone had the kind of week that makes you capitalize his name in the team logo. Luka Doncic scored a season-high 51 points against the Bulls on March 12, adding 10 rebounds and 9 assists in a 142-130 win, then capped the week with a fadeaway game-winner with 0.5 seconds left in overtime to beat the Nuggets 127-125 on March 14 -- finishing that one with a 30-point triple-double. The 51-point eruption translated to 99.8 fantasy points, the highest single-game score across the entire league this week and the engine behind Luka's 234.55 FP in just 3 games (78.18 FPPG). LeBron James summed it up postgame: Nick's star is playing at a generational level, and the Lakers -- now winners of five straight -- are climbing in the crowded Western Conference standings.
  • SGA Breaks Wilt Chamberlain's 63-Year Consecutive 20-Point Scoring Record
    Hayden's other franchise pillar made history on March 12 when Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 35 points in Oklahoma City's 104-102 win over Boston, extending his streak of consecutive games with at least 20 points to 127 -- breaking Wilt Chamberlain's record that had stood since 1963. SGA reached the milestone on a 20-foot jumper in the third quarter, drawing a standing ovation and MVP chants from the Paycom Center crowd. For Hayden, SGA's consistency has been a lifeline in a season defined by bad luck: his 189.80 FP across 3 games this week (including an 81.55 FP performance against Denver) made him the second-best contributor on a team that pulled off a massive upset. The Thunder are 103-24 during the streak, and SGA shows no signs of slowing down as the NBA's reigning MVP enters the final stretch of the regular season.
  • Bam Adebayo Scores 83 Points -- Second-Most in NBA History
    Benton's Bam Adebayo turned in one of the most surreal performances in NBA history on March 10, scoring 83 points in Miami's 150-129 win over Washington -- the second-highest single-game total ever, trailing only Wilt Chamberlain's mythical 100. Adebayo poured in 31 points in the first quarter alone, had 43 by halftime, and surpassed Kobe Bryant's 81-point mark with free throws in the final minutes. The performance generated both celebration and controversy -- he set NBA records with 36 made free throws on 43 attempts, and the Heat intentionally fouled late to extend possessions for their star. In fantasy terms, the 83-point explosion translated to an 88.40 FP outing that ranks as the week's third-best individual game. The Heat have now won six straight and sit at 37-29, with Adebayo emerging as a genuine MVP candidate alongside a roster that was missing Tyler Herro, Norman Powell, and Andrew Wiggins.
  • Curry Ruled Out Another 10 Days as Knee Issues Linger
    Garrett's Stephen Curry remains sidelined with patellofemoral pain syndrome -- commonly known as runner's knee -- after the Warriors announced on March 11 that he'll miss at least another 10 days and be re-evaluated around March 21. Curry has now missed 17 consecutive games since January 30, and the Warriors revealed he also has a bone bruise complicating the recovery. He's begun on-court individual workouts, which is progress, but head coach Steve Kerr acknowledged that a specific ramp-up process with full-speed scrimmages is still required before clearance. For Garrett, the absence has been devastating: Curry's 42.27 season FPPG ranks 13th among all rostered players, and his slot in the lineup remains unfilled heading into the final week of the fantasy regular season. Golden State has gone 5-11 during this stretch and slipped in the Western Conference standings.
  • Jokic Rattles Off Three Straight Triple-Doubles to Close the Week
    Nikola Jokic capped an extraordinary week for Hayden by recording triple-doubles in all three of his games from March 10-14. He logged 32 points, 14 rebounds, and 13 assists in a narrow loss to the Thunder on March 10, followed by a breezy 16-12-13 line in a blowout win over Houston, then delivered the headliner: 31 points, 20 rebounds, and 12 assists to lead a 20-point comeback against San Antonio on March 12 -- snapping the Spurs' five-game win streak. He then closed with another dominant outing against the Lakers. With 27 triple-doubles on the season -- far and away the league lead -- Jokic is playing the best basketball on the planet. His 304.35 FP across 4 games earned him his sixth POTW award this season, and at 62.22 FPPG for the year, no other rostered player is in the same stratosphere.

Rumor Mill

Trade Ideas

  • Hayden sends Tyrese Haliburton <-> Garrett sends Giannis Antetokounmpo
    A straightforward swap that addresses both teams' needs heading into the offseason. Hayden gets a center with genuine top-10 upside in Giannis (40.93 season FPPG despite a 53.7% games played rate), while Garrett takes on the Haliburton contract knowing the guard has been out all season. With Hayden already owning both the 1st and 2nd overall picks in the 2026 draft, adding Giannis to a keeper core of Jokic, SGA, and two premium draft selections would create the league's most loaded roster on paper. Hayden and Garrett have traded 3 times all-time, making this a relatively rare trade corridor.
  • Nick sends Amen Thompson + Chet Holmgren <-> Garrett sends Victor Wembanyama
    A consolidation play that would give Nick the league's ultimate center weapon in Wembanyama (49.15 season FPPG, 55.78 projected FPPG rest of season) while Garrett gets two young building blocks in Thompson (34.32 FPPG, 97.0% games played) and Holmgren (35.04 FPPG). For the six-time champion, the math is simple: Wemby at 21 years old is a dynasty-defining keeper who could anchor Nick's lineup for a decade. For Garrett, the two-for-one provides roster flexibility and youth, especially with Sabonis out for the season. Nick and Garrett have made 6 deals all-time, including this season's Week 7 KAT trade.
  • Benton sends Jayson Tatum <-> Hayden sends Scottie Barnes
    A buy-low gamble for Benton, who would acquire a player in Barnes (39.10 season FPPG, 97.0% games played) who has underperformed his projections but offers elite durability -- something Benton desperately needs with Tatum having played just 6.0% of available games this season. Hayden gets the upside play in Tatum (40.50 FPPG when healthy, 39.46 projected FPPG rest of season), betting that a full offseason of recovery produces a top-15 keeper. Benton and Hayden have completed 5 deals all-time, and with both teams locked into their finishing positions, this is a pure offseason positioning move.
  • Nick sends Karl-Anthony Towns + Nickeil Alexander-Walker <-> Garrett sends Donovan Clingan
    Another consolidation play for Nick, swapping two solid contributors for Clingan (35.40 FPPG, 92.6% games played), whose age-21 upside and 10.6% efficiency overperformance make him an intriguing long-term center option. Towns (38.79 FPPG) provides immediate production for Garrett but turns 31 in November, while NAW (33.26 FPPG) adds backcourt depth. This would be the second Nick-Garrett deal this season, building on the Week 7 trade that originally sent Towns to Nick.

Free Agent Targets

  • Darius Garland (PG) -> Hayden: 36.10 proj FPPG. Addresses Hayden's injury-depleted guard corps with the final week of the regular season looming and a playoff matchup against Garrett on deck.
  • Zion Williamson (SF,PF,C) -> Hayden: 31.48 proj FPPG. Addresses Hayden's injury-depleted forward corps -- with Siakam and Markkanen still working back, Zion provides a warm body at a thin position.
  • Dylan Cardwell (PF,C) -> Benton: 29.75 proj FPPG. Addresses Benton's center shortage with Jarrett Allen still sidelined and the playoff matchup against Garrett requiring depth up front.
  • Draymond Green (PF,C) -> Garrett: 28.80 proj FPPG. Addresses Garrett's center corps with Sabonis done for the season and Curry's absence stretching the rotation thin.
  • Jrue Holiday (PG,SG) -> Nick: 34.47 proj FPPG. Addresses Nick's guard depth with Anthony Davis still out -- Holiday's veteran production could provide a boost for the playoff opener against Hayden.

Hot Streaks

  • Matas Buzelis (Hayden): +52.4% above projection over the last 4 weeks, +52.4% over the last 14 days. Sell high candidate -- actual 44.4 FPPG is unlikely to sustain for a rookie averaging 29.97 on the season.
  • Josh Giddey (Benton): +37.8% above projection over the last 4 weeks, +53.6% over the last 14 days. Sell high candidate -- actual 52.8 FPPG is unlikely to sustain, but the trend is still climbing. May have more room to run.
  • Donovan Clingan (Garrett): +32.7% above projection over the last 4 weeks, +26.5% over the last 14 days. Sell high candidate -- actual 42.5 FPPG is unlikely to sustain, and the hot streak is cooling slightly.
  • Kyle Filipowski (Garrett): +30.8% above projection over the last 4 weeks, +30.8% over the last 14 days. Sell high candidate -- actual 40.6 FPPG is unlikely to sustain for a player averaging 24.73 on the season.
  • Kon Knueppel (Hayden): +27.3% above projection over the last 4 weeks, +15.0% over the last 14 days. Sell high candidate -- actual 37.5 FPPG is unlikely to sustain, and the hot streak is cooling off. Sell window may be closing.
  • Lauri Markkanen (Hayden): +17.9% above projection over the last 4 weeks. Elevated trade value -- producing 30.9 FPPG vs 26.2 projected.

Slump Watch

  • Rudy Gobert (Garrett): -30.9% below projection over the last 4 weeks, -30.9% over the last 14 days. Gobert's 20.74 FPPG this week was the worst on Garrett's roster, and the sustained underperformance is concerning heading into the playoffs. No clear upgrade available on waivers -- hold for now despite the struggles.
  • Alperen Sengun (Garrett): -21.7% below projection over the last 4 weeks, -40.4% over the last 14 days. The decline is accelerating, not improving, which is an alarming trend for a player Garrett needs in the playoff push. No clear replacement available.
  • Scottie Barnes (Hayden): -18.9% below projection over the last 4 weeks, -18.8% over the last 14 days. Consistent underperformance without improvement. At 39.10 season FPPG, Barnes is still a hold given his 97.0% availability, but the production gap is real. No clear replacement available.
  • Julius Randle (Benton): -15.1% below projection over the last 4 weeks, -23.1% over the last 14 days. The slump is deepening -- Randle's 8.05 FP game against the Warriors this week was the league's worst individual performance. No clear replacement available.
  • Giannis Antetokounmpo (Garrett): -25.1% below projection over the last 4 weeks, -25.1% over the last 14 days. When healthy, Giannis is a top-5 fantasy asset (40.93 FPPG), but the 53.7% games played rate and recent underperformance make him a frustrating hold. No clear replacement available.
End of Newsletter
CHS Alumni Fantasy Basketball League
